purpose: Analyze Android intent-filter descriptors from manifest data and report deep link exposure, browsable activities, and missing host constraints.

endpoints.description: Analyze normalized Android component intent filters for risky deep link exposure.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Android intent-filter risk analyzer for manifest-derived component metadata.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after android.manifest_analyzer or another manifest parser extracts component intent filters.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use for runtime Android intent dispatch simulation or APK bytecode analysis.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Parse manifest components. 2) Submit normalized components to /android/intents/analyze. 3) Review browsable and exported deep link findings.

findings.message: Browsable deep link does not declare a host.

failure_modes.condition: Intent filter descriptor is missing expected arrays
failure_modes.recovery: Normalize manifest parser output before analysis

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls
Must not execute Android build tools or APK installers

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Treat each component intent_filters item as normalized data with actions, categories, and data arrays. Flag exported activities with BROWSABLE and VIEW. Flag https/http data entries missing host.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Manifest parsers often output a single object instead of an array. This module expects normalized arrays and should return 400 if shape is not normalized.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Keep analyzer independent from XML parsing so it composes with android.manifest_analyzer.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not call adb, aapt, gradle, or apktool, and do not infer runtime behavior beyond manifest descriptors.

test_cases.name: browsable deep link without host warns
